title: Herby Melon Salad with Grated Halloumi
summary: Grated halloumi that melts into every bite takes this salad to the next level, its salty richness is the perfect combo with juicy melon. It's the ultimate no cook meal.

method.description: Zest half the lemon and set aside.

Add the pumpkin seeds, mustard, olive oil and lemon juice into a blender. Blend until creamy and smooth. No blender? Just finely chop the pumpkin seeds and add them into a bowl with the lemon juice, slowly whisk in the olive oil until emulsified. Taste and season with salt, sugar, pepper and sherry vinegar, you can adjust the acidity by adding a little more vinegar or sugar.
Drain and rinse the cannellini beans. Chop the cucumber and melon into bite-sized chunks. Pick the mint and parsley leaves, discarding the stalks.
Chuck all the herbs, cucumber, melon and crutons into a big bowl with the dressing and lemon zest, toss well. Divide between plates, then finely grate the halloumi over the top to finish. Enjoy!

tips: Any melon works - honeydew, cantaloupe or watermelon are all great.
Grate the halloumi at the last minute for maximum impact.
Serve it cold for a super refreshing lunch or dinner.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
